plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it is in a downtrend, while maintaining some income exposure.
Initiate small positions in NOW and ANET, both high-scoring quality setups, for diversification.
This plan aims for capital preservation by reducing leverage and concentration.
A significant portion of the book will be held as cash after these sales.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering overall leverage. Expect lower volatility and drawdown potential, but also limited upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the concentration mandate and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its below-stop status and high leverage, reducing risk.
Trim SPAL to a small 2% to maintain minimal leveraged exposure within limits.
Reduce QQQI to 10% given its downtrend, while keeping some income exposure.
Initiate positions in NOW and ANET, both high-scoring quality tech setups, for growth.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth by diversifying into quality names.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed, leading to a more stable portfolio. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown, with a reasonable potential for growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the concentration mandate, freeing up capital.
Sell SOXL entirely due to being below stop; reallocate funds to stronger setups.
Trim QQQI to 10% due to its downtrend, shifting focus to high-growth opportunities.
Initiate larger positions in NOW and ANET, both top-scoring growth setups.
Add NVDU, another high-scoring setup, to further concentrate in strong trends.
This plan leans into growth by concentrating in high-score setups and managing leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It involves higher concentration and potential for increased leverage, leading to significantly higher upside potential but also much larger drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL decay daily, amplifying losses if not managed with strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and free up capital for income.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, prioritizing stability.
Increase QQQI to 30% to boost monthly distributions and income exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift focus from growth to income and lower volatility.
Initiate a small position in XOM, a stable name with a watch score, for diversification.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1%/month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall. Expect lower volatility, but also limited capital appreciation.
